Diodia punicea, also known as the Red button plant, is a perennial herb native to the tropical regions of Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, with health benefits including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Diodia punicea include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties.
